163: // fullInstanceRecord returns an instanceRecord with every field set to a
164: // non-zero/non-default value.  Used by round-trip tests to verify that
165: // removeInstanceFromState and updateInstanceInState do not silently drop any
166: // field when they deserialise and re-serialise the state JSON.
167: func fullInstanceRecord(title string) instanceRecord {
168: 	return instanceRecord{
169: 		Title:                  title,
170: 		Path:                   "/worktrees/" + title,
171: 		Branch:                 "feat/" + title,
172: 		Status:                 instanceRunning,
173: 		Height:                 48,
174: 		Width:                  220,
175: 		CreatedAt:              time.Date(2026, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
176: 		UpdatedAt:              time.Date(2026, 1, 2, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
177: 		Program:                "claude",
178: 		AutoYes:                true,
179: 		SkipPermissions:        true,
180: 		TaskFile:               title + ".md",
181: 		AgentType:              "coder",
182: 		TaskNumber:             3,
183: 		WaveNumber:             2,
184: 		PeerCount:              4,
185: 		IsReviewer:             false,
186: 		ImplementationComplete: true,
187: 		SoloAgent:              false,
188: 		QueuedPrompt:           "hello from " + title,
189: 		ReviewCycle:            2,
190: 		Worktree: instanceWorktree{
191: 			RepoPath:      "/repo",
192: 			WorktreePath:  "/repo/.worktrees/" + title,
193: 			SessionName:   title,
194: 			BranchName:    "feat/" + title,
195: 			BaseCommitSHA: "deadbeef" + title,
196: 		},
197: 		DiffStats: instanceDiffStats{
198: 			Added:   10,
199: 			Removed: 5,
200: 			Content: "some diff for " + title,
201: 		},
202: 	}
203: }

241: // does not corrupt any fields of the remaining instances during the JSON round-trip.
242: // This is the regression test for the data-loss bug where instanceRecord was
243: // incomplete and silently dropped ~15 fields from every surviving instance.
244: func TestRemoveInstanceFromState_PreservesFields(t *testing.T) {

254: 	require.NoError(t, err)

256:

279: // TestUpdateInstanceInState_PreservesFields verifies that updating one field of one
280: // instance does not corrupt any fields of the other instances during the JSON
281: // round-trip.  This is the regression test for the data-loss bug in pause/resume.
282: func TestUpdateInstanceInState_PreservesFields(t *testing.T) {
283: 	untouched := fullInstanceRecord("untouched")
284: 	target := fullInstanceRecord("target")
285: 	target.Status = instancePaused // start paused so we can "resume" it
286: 
287: 	state := newTestStateFromRecords(t, []instanceRecord{untouched, target})
288: 
289: 	// Simulate what pause does: sets status to paused and clears the worktree path.
290: 	err := updateInstanceInState(state, "target", func(r *instanceRecord) error {
291: 		r.Status = instancePaused
292: 		r.Worktree.WorktreePath = ""
293: 		return nil
294: 	})
295: 	require.NoError(t, err)
296: 
297: 	records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
298: 	require.NoError(t, err)
299: 	require.Len(t, records, 2)
300: 
301: 	// untouched instance must have ALL its fields preserved.
302: 	var gotUntouched, gotTarget instanceRecord
303: 	for _, r := range records {
304: 		switch r.Title {
305: 		case "untouched":
306: 			gotUntouched = r
307: 		case "target":
308: 			gotTarget = r
309: 		}
310: 	}
311: 
312: 	assertRecordFieldsEqual(t, untouched, gotUntouched)
313: 
314: 	// target must have only Status and Worktree.WorktreePath changed.
315: 	assert.Equal(t, instancePaused, gotTarget.Status, "target.Status should be paused")
316: 	assert.Empty(t, gotTarget.Worktree.WorktreePath, "target.Worktree.WorktreePath should be cleared")
317: 	// All other fields of target should be preserved.
318: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Height, gotTarget.Height, "target.Height")
319: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Width, gotTarget.Width, "target.Width")
320: 	assert.Equal(t, target.AutoYes, gotTarget.AutoYes, "target.AutoYes")
321: 	assert.Equal(t, target.SkipPermissions, gotTarget.SkipPermissions, "target.SkipPermissions")
322: 	assert.Equal(t, target.TaskNumber, gotTarget.TaskNumber, "target.TaskNumber")
323: 	assert.Equal(t, target.WaveNumber, gotTarget.WaveNumber, "target.WaveNumber")
324: 	assert.Equal(t, target.PeerCount, gotTarget.PeerCount, "target.PeerCount")
325: 	assert.Equal(t, target.ImplementationComplete, gotTarget.ImplementationComplete, "target.ImplementationComplete")
326: 	assert.Equal(t, target.QueuedPrompt, gotTarget.QueuedPrompt, "target.QueuedPrompt")
327: 	assert.Equal(t, target.ReviewCycle, gotTarget.ReviewCycle, "target.ReviewCycle")
328: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.RepoPath, gotTarget.Worktree.RepoPath, "target.Worktree.RepoPath")
329: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.SessionName, gotTarget.Worktree.SessionName, "target.Worktree.SessionName")
330: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.BranchName, gotTarget.Worktree.BranchName, "target.Worktree.BranchName")
331: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA, gotTarget.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA, "target.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA")
332: 	assert.Equal(t, target.DiffStats.Added, gotTarget.DiffStats.Added, "target.DiffStats.Added")
333: 	assert.Equal(t, target.DiffStats.Removed, gotTarget.DiffStats.Removed, "target.DiffStats.Removed")
334: 	assert.Equal(t, target.DiffStats.Content, gotTarget.DiffStats.Content, "target.DiffStats.Content")
335: }

20: // executeTaskRegister registers a plan file into the task store. The filePath
21: // is resolved relative to the caller's working directory.
22: func executeTaskRegister(project, filePath, branch, topic, description string, store taskstore.Store) error {
23: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(filePath)
24: 	if err != nil {
25: 		return fmt.Errorf("task file not found: %s", filePath)
26: 	}
27: 	ps, err := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
28: 	if err != nil {
29: 		return err
30: 	}
31: 	planFile := filepath.Base(filePath)
32: 	if description == "" {
33: 		description = strings.TrimSuffix(planFile, ".md")
34: 		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(data), "\n") {
35: 			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "# ") {
36: 				description = strings.TrimPrefix(line, "# ")
37: 				break
38: 			}
39: 		}
40: 	}
41: 	if branch == "" {
42: 		slug := strings.TrimSuffix(planFile, ".md")
43: 		branch = "plan/" + slug
44: 	}
45: 	info, _ := os.Stat(filePath)
46: 	createdAt := info.ModTime()
47: 	return ps.CreateWithContent(planFile, description, branch, topic, createdAt, string(data))
48: }
49: 
50: // executeTaskList returns a formatted string listing all plans, optionally
51: // filtered by status. Exported for testing without cobra plumbing.
52: func executeTaskList(project, statusFilter string, store taskstore.Store) string {
53: 	ps, err := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
54: 	if err != nil {
55: 		return fmt.Sprintf("error: %v", err)
56: 	}
57: 	var sb strings.Builder
58: 	for _, info := range ps.List() {
59: 		if statusFilter != "" && string(info.Status) != statusFilter {
60: 			continue
61: 		}
62: 		line := fmt.Sprintf("%-14s %-50s %s", info.Status, info.Filename, info.Branch)
63: 		sb.WriteString(strings.TrimRight(line, " ") + "\n")
64: 	}
65: 	return sb.String()
66: }
67: 
68: // executeTaskListWithStore returns a formatted string listing all plans from a
69: // remote store backend. storeURL is the base URL of the task store server
70: // (e.g. "http://athena:7433") and project is the project name to query.
71: func executeTaskListWithStore(storeURL, project string) string {
72: 	store := taskstore.NewHTTPStore(storeURL, project)
73: 	ps, err := taskstate.Load(store, project, "")
74: 	if err != nil {
75: 		return fmt.Sprintf("error: %v", err)
76: 	}
77: 	var sb strings.Builder
78: 	for _, info := range ps.List() {
79: 		line := fmt.Sprintf("%-14s %-50s %s", info.Status, info.Filename, info.Branch)
80: 		sb.WriteString(strings.TrimRight(line, " ") + "\n")
81: 	}
82: 	return sb.String()
83: }

85: // executeTaskSetStatus force-overrides a plan's status, bypassing the FSM.
86: // Requires force=true to prevent accidental misuse.
87: func executeTaskSetStatus(project, planFile, status string, force bool, store taskstore.Store) error {
88: 	if !force {
89: 		return fmt.Errorf("--force required to override task status (this bypasses the FSM)")
90: 	}
91: 	ps, err := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
92: 	if err != nil {
93: 		return err
94: 	}
95: 	return ps.ForceSetStatus(planFile, taskstate.Status(status))
96: }

98: // executeTaskTransition applies a named FSM event to a plan and returns the new status.
99: func executeTaskTransition(project, planFile, event string, store taskstore.Store) (string, error) {
100: 	eventMap := map[string]taskfsm.Event{
101: 		"plan_start":         taskfsm.PlanStart,
102: 		"planner_finished":   taskfsm.PlannerFinished,
103: 		"implement_start":    taskfsm.ImplementStart,
104: 		"implement_finished": taskfsm.ImplementFinished,
105: 		"review_approved":    taskfsm.ReviewApproved,
106: 		"review_changes":     taskfsm.ReviewChangesRequested,
107: 		"request_review":     taskfsm.RequestReview,
108: 		"start_over":         taskfsm.StartOver,
109: 		"reimplement":        taskfsm.Reimplement,
110: 		"cancel":             taskfsm.Cancel,
111: 		"reopen":             taskfsm.Reopen,
112: 	}
113: 	fsmEvent, ok := eventMap[event]
114: 	if !ok {
115: 		names := make([]string, 0, len(eventMap))
116: 		for k := range eventMap {
117: 			names = append(names, k)
118: 		}
119: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("unknown event %q; valid events: %s", event, strings.Join(names, ", "))
120: 	}
121: 	fsm := newFSMByProject(project, store)
122: 	if err := fsm.Transition(planFile, fsmEvent); err != nil {
123: 		return "", err
124: 	}
125: 	ps, err := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
126: 	if err != nil {
127: 		return "", err
128: 	}
129: 	entry, _ := ps.Entry(planFile)
130: 	return string(entry.Status), nil
131: }
132: 
133: // executeTaskImplement transitions a plan into implementing state and writes
134: // a wave signal file so the TUI metadata tick can pick it up.
135: func executeTaskImplement(repoRoot, project, planFile string, wave int, store taskstore.Store) error {
136: 	if wave < 1 {
137: 		return fmt.Errorf("wave number must be >= 1, got %d", wave)
138: 	}
139: 	fsm := newFSMByProject(project, store)
140: 	ps, err := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
141: 	if err != nil {
142: 		return err
143: 	}
144: 	entry, ok := ps.Entry(planFile)
145: 	if !ok {
146: 		return fmt.Errorf("task not found: %s", planFile)
147: 	}
148: 	current := taskfsm.Status(entry.Status)
149: 	// If still in planning, finish that phase first (→ ready).
150: 	if current == taskfsm.StatusPlanning {
151: 		if err := fsm.Transition(planFile, taskfsm.PlannerFinished); err != nil {
152: 			return err
153: 		}
154: 		current = taskfsm.StatusReady
155: 	}
156: 	// Advance to implementing unless already there.
157: 	if current != taskfsm.StatusImplementing {
158: 		if err := fsm.Transition(planFile, taskfsm.ImplementStart); err != nil {
159: 			return err
160: 		}
161: 	}
162: 
163: 	// Write the wave signal file consumed by the TUI metadata tick.
164: 	signalsDir := filepath.Join(repoRoot, ".kasmos", "signals")
165: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(signalsDir, 0o755); err != nil {
166: 		return err
167: 	}
168: 	signalName := fmt.Sprintf("implement-wave-%d-%s", wave, planFile)
169: 	return os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(signalsDir, signalName), nil, 0o644)
170: }
171: 
172: // executeTaskShow retrieves plan content from the task store and returns it
173: // as raw markdown. Returns an error if the plan doesn't exist or has no content.
174: func executeTaskShow(project, planFile string, store taskstore.Store) (string, error) {
175: 	ps, err := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
176: 	if err != nil {
177: 		return "", err
178: 	}
179: 	if _, ok := ps.Entry(planFile); !ok {
180: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("task not found: %s", planFile)
181: 	}
182: 	content, err := ps.GetContent(planFile)
183: 	if err != nil {
184: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("get content for %s: %w", planFile, err)
185: 	}
186: 	if strings.TrimSpace(content) == "" {
187: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("no content stored for %s", planFile)
188: 	}
189: 	return content, nil
190: }
191: 
192: // executeTaskLinkClickUp iterates all plans in the given project, reads their
193: // content, parses the ClickUp task ID from the "**Source:** ClickUp <ID>" line,
194: // and stores it in the clickup_task_id field for any plan that has an ID in its
195: // content but not yet in the store. Returns the count of plans updated.
196: func executeTaskLinkClickUp(project string, store taskstore.Store) (int, error) {
197: 	plans, err := store.List(project)
198: 	if err != nil {
199: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("list tasks: %w", err)
200: 	}
201: 
202: 	updated := 0
203: 	for _, plan := range plans {
204: 		// Skip plans that already have a ClickUp task ID.
205: 		if plan.ClickUpTaskID != "" {
206: 			continue
207: 		}
208: 
209: 		content, err := store.GetContent(project, plan.Filename)
210: 		if err != nil {
211: 			// Non-fatal: skip plans whose content can't be read.
212: 			continue
213: 		}
214: 
215: 		taskID := clickup.ParseClickUpTaskID(content)
216: 		if taskID == "" {
217: 			continue
218: 		}
219: 
220: 		if err := store.SetClickUpTaskID(project, plan.Filename, taskID); err != nil {
221: 			return updated, fmt.Errorf("set clickup task id for %s: %w", plan.Filename, err)
222: 		}
223: 		updated++
224: 	}
225: 
226: 	return updated, nil
227: }
228: 
229: // resolveTaskEntry loads task state for the given project, looks up the entry
230: // by filename, and backfills the branch name if it is empty (using
231: // git.TaskBranchFromFile). Returns an error if the entry is not found.
232: func resolveTaskEntry(project, filename string, store taskstore.Store) (taskstate.TaskEntry, error) {
233: 	ps, err := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
234: 	if err != nil {
235: 		return taskstate.TaskEntry{}, err
236: 	}
237: 	entry, ok := ps.Entry(filename)
238: 	if !ok {
239: 		return taskstate.TaskEntry{}, fmt.Errorf("task not found: %s", filename)
240: 	}
241: 	if entry.Branch == "" {
242: 		entry.Branch = git.TaskBranchFromFile(filename)
243: 	}
244: 	return entry, nil
245: }
246: 
247: // executeTaskCreate creates a new task entry in the store. name is the plan
248: // name without the .md extension. branch defaults to "plan/<name>" when empty.
249: // If content is non-empty, it is stored alongside the metadata.
250: func executeTaskCreate(project, name, description, branch, topic, content string, store taskstore.Store) error {
251: 	filename := name + ".md"
252: 	if branch == "" {
253: 		branch = "plan/" + name
254: 	}
255: 	ps, err := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
256: 	if err != nil {
257: 		return err
258: 	}
259: 	createdAt := time.Now()
260: 	if content != "" {
261: 		return ps.CreateWithContent(filename, description, branch, topic, createdAt, content)
262: 	}
263: 	return ps.Create(filename, description, branch, topic, createdAt)
264: }
265: 
266: // executeTaskStart transitions a plan to implementing status and sets up the
267: // git branch + worktree. It walks planning → ready → implementing via the FSM
268: // if the plan is currently in planning state. Returns the worktree path.
269: func executeTaskStart(repoRoot, project, planFile string, store taskstore.Store) (string, error) {
270: 	fsm := newFSMByProject(project, store)
271: 
272: 	ps, err := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
273: 	if err != nil {
274: 		return "", err
275: 	}
276: 	entry, ok := ps.Entry(planFile)
277: 	if !ok {
278: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("task not found: %s", planFile)
279: 	}
280: 
281: 	current := taskfsm.Status(entry.Status)
282: 	// Walk planning → ready first if needed.
283: 	if current == taskfsm.StatusPlanning {
284: 		if err := fsm.Transition(planFile, taskfsm.PlannerFinished); err != nil {
285: 			return "", err
286: 		}
287: 		current = taskfsm.StatusReady
288: 	}
289: 	// Advance to implementing.
290: 	if current != taskfsm.StatusImplementing {
291: 		if err := fsm.Transition(planFile, taskfsm.ImplementStart); err != nil {
292: 			return "", err
293: 		}
294: 	}
295: 
296: 	// Resolve branch — backfill if not set.
297: 	branch := entry.Branch
298: 	if branch == "" {
299: 		branch = git.TaskBranchFromFile(planFile)
300: 	}
301: 
302: 	// Set up the git branch and worktree.
303: 	if err := git.EnsureTaskBranch(repoRoot, branch); err != nil {
304: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("ensure task branch: %w", err)
305: 	}
306: 	wt := git.NewSharedTaskWorktree(repoRoot, branch)
307: 	if err := wt.Setup(); err != nil {
308: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("setup worktree: %w", err)
309: 	}
310: 	return wt.GetWorktreePath(), nil
311: }
312: 
313: // executeTaskPush resolves the task entry and its branch, constructs a
314: // GitWorktree from stored state, commits any dirty changes, and pushes to
315: // origin. The commit message defaults to "update from kas" when empty.
316: func executeTaskPush(repoRoot, project, planFile, message string, store taskstore.Store) error {
317: 	if message == "" {
318: 		message = "update from kas"
319: 	}
320: 	entry, err := resolveTaskEntry(project, planFile, store)
321: 	if err != nil {
322: 		return err
323: 	}
324: 	branch := entry.Branch
325: 	worktreePath := git.TaskWorktreePath(repoRoot, branch)
326: 	wt := git.NewGitWorktreeFromStorage(repoRoot, worktreePath, "push", branch, "")
327: 	return wt.PushChanges(message, false)
328: }
329: 
330: // executeTaskMerge merges the plan branch into the current branch (typically
331: // main), then walks the FSM to done. If the task is not yet in reviewing state,
332: // it transitions through ImplementFinished first. Returns an error if the git
333: // merge fails.
334: func executeTaskMerge(repoRoot, project, planFile string, store taskstore.Store) error {
335: 	entry, err := resolveTaskEntry(project, planFile, store)
336: 	if err != nil {
337: 		return err
338: 	}
339: 	branch := entry.Branch
340: 
341: 	// Validate repoRoot before attempting git operations.
342: 	if _, serr := os.Stat(repoRoot); serr != nil {
343: 		return fmt.Errorf("invalid repo root %q: %w", repoRoot, serr)
344: 	}
345: 
346: 	// Git merge first — only advance the FSM on success.
347: 	if err := git.MergeTaskBranch(repoRoot, branch); err != nil {
348: 		return err
349: 	}
350: 
351: 	// Walk FSM to done.
352: 	fsm := newFSMByProject(project, store)
353: 	current := taskfsm.Status(entry.Status)
354: 	if current != taskfsm.StatusReviewing {
355: 		if current == taskfsm.StatusImplementing {
356: 			if err := fsm.Transition(planFile, taskfsm.ImplementFinished); err != nil {
357: 				return err
358: 			}
359: 		} else {
360: 			// Force to reviewing so ReviewApproved can proceed.
361: 			ps, lerr := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
362: 			if lerr != nil {
363: 				return lerr
364: 			}
365: 			if ferr := ps.ForceSetStatus(planFile, taskstate.StatusReviewing); ferr != nil {
366: 				return ferr
367: 			}
368: 		}
369: 	}
370: 	return fsm.Transition(planFile, taskfsm.ReviewApproved)
371: }
372: 
373: // executeTaskStartOver removes the plan worktree, deletes and recreates the
374: // branch from HEAD (via git.ResetTaskBranch), then transitions the FSM to
375: // planning. Uses StartOver FSM event for states where it is valid; falls back
376: // to ForceSetStatus for all other states.
377: func executeTaskStartOver(repoRoot, project, planFile string, store taskstore.Store) error {
378: 	entry, err := resolveTaskEntry(project, planFile, store)
379: 	if err != nil {
380: 		return err
381: 	}
382: 	branch := entry.Branch
383: 
384: 	// Validate repoRoot before attempting git operations.
385: 	if _, serr := os.Stat(repoRoot); serr != nil {
386: 		return fmt.Errorf("invalid repo root %q: %w", repoRoot, serr)
387: 	}
388: 
389: 	// Git reset first — only touch FSM on success.
390: 	if err := git.ResetTaskBranch(repoRoot, branch); err != nil {
391: 		return err
392: 	}
393: 
394: 	// Try the FSM StartOver event; fall back to ForceSetStatus if not valid
395: 	// from the current state (StartOver is only defined from done in the FSM).
396: 	fsm := newFSMByProject(project, store)
397: 	if ferr := fsm.Transition(planFile, taskfsm.StartOver); ferr != nil {
398: 		ps, lerr := loadTaskStateByProject(project, store)
399: 		if lerr != nil {
400: 			return lerr
401: 		}
402: 		return ps.ForceSetStatus(planFile, taskstate.StatusPlanning)
403: 	}
404: 	return nil
405: }
406: 
407: // executeTaskPR resolves the task entry, derives the PR title from the task
408: // description when title is empty, generates a PR body from the git log, and
409: // creates (or reopens) the PR via the GitHub CLI. The PR URL is printed to
410: // stdout by the gh CLI; the returned string is currently always empty.
411: func executeTaskPR(repoRoot, project, planFile, title string, store taskstore.Store) (string, error) {
412: 	entry, err := resolveTaskEntry(project, planFile, store)
413: 	if err != nil {
414: 		return "", err
415: 	}
416: 	branch := entry.Branch
417: 	if title == "" {
418: 		title = entry.Description
419: 	}
420: 	if title == "" {
421: 		// Last-resort: use the filename stem as the title.
422: 		title = strings.TrimSuffix(planFile, ".md")
423: 	}
424: 	worktreePath := git.TaskWorktreePath(repoRoot, branch)
425: 	wt := git.NewGitWorktreeFromStorage(repoRoot, worktreePath, "pr", branch, "")
426: 	body, _ := wt.GeneratePRBody()
427: 	if err := wt.CreatePR(title, body, "update from kas"); err != nil {
428: 		return "", err
429: 	}
430: 	return "", nil
431: }

655: 	// kas task push

769: 	planCmd.AddCommand(linkClickUpCmd)
770: 
771: 	return planCmd
772: }
773: 
774: // resolveStoreConfig returns the remote store and project name from config.
775: // Returns (nil, "") when no remote store is configured.
776: func resolveStoreConfig(project string) (taskstore.Store, string) {
777: 	cfg := config.LoadConfig()
778: 	if cfg.DatabaseURL == "" {
779: 		return nil, ""
780: 	}
781: 	store, err := taskstore.NewStoreFromConfig(cfg.DatabaseURL, project)
782: 	if err != nil || store == nil {
783: 		return nil, ""
784: 	}
785: 	return store, project
786: }
787: 
788: // localSQLiteStore opens (or creates) the local SQLite task store at the
789: // canonical path returned by taskstore.ResolvedDBPath(). Used as a fallback
790: // when no remote store is configured.
791: func localSQLiteStore() (taskstore.Store, error) {
792: 	dbPath := taskstore.ResolvedDBPath()
793: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dbPath), 0o755); err != nil {
794: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create kasmos config dir: %w", err)
795: 	}
796: 	return taskstore.NewSQLiteStore(dbPath)
797: }
798: 
799: // loadTaskStateByProject loads task state using the store backend and a project name.
800: // When store is nil, falls back to the local SQLite store.
801: func loadTaskStateByProject(project string, store taskstore.Store) (*taskstate.TaskState, error) {
802: 	if store == nil {
803: 		var err error
804: 		store, err = localSQLiteStore()
805: 		if err != nil {
806: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("open local task store: %w", err)
807: 		}
808: 	}
809: 	return taskstate.Load(store, project, "")
810: }
811: 
812: // newFSMByProject creates a TaskStateMachine backed by the given store and project name.
813: // When store is nil, falls back to the local SQLite store.
814: func newFSMByProject(project string, store taskstore.Store) *taskfsm.TaskStateMachine {
815: 	if store == nil {
816: 		var err error
817: 		store, err = localSQLiteStore()
818: 		if err != nil {
819: 			panic("newFSMByProject: open local task store: " + err.Error())
820: 		}
821: 	}
822: 	return taskfsm.New(store, project, "")
823: }
824: 
825: // resolveStore returns the remote task store from config, or nil if not
826: // configured or unreachable.
827: func resolveStore(project string) taskstore.Store {
828: 	store, _ := resolveStoreConfig(project)
829: 	if store != nil && store.Ping() == nil {
830: 		return store
831: 	}
832: 	return nil
833: }
834: 
835: // resolveRepoInfo resolves the main repository root and derives the project
836: // name from it. Handles both regular repos and git worktrees. The project name
837: // is the basename of the repo root directory (e.g. "kasmos" for /home/kas/dev/kasmos).
838: func resolveRepoInfo() (repoRoot, project string, err error) {
839: 	cwd, err := os.Getwd()
840: 	if err != nil {
841: 		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("get cwd: %w", err)
842: 	}
843: 
844: 	root, err := resolveRepoRoot(cwd)
845: 	if err != nil {
846: 		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot resolve repo root: %w", err)
847: 	}
848: 	return root, filepath.Base(root), nil
849: }
850: 
851: // resolveRepoRoot returns the root directory of the git repository that owns
852: // dir. It handles both regular repositories (where .git is a directory) and
853: // git worktrees (where .git is a file with a "gitdir:" pointer). On failure it
854: // falls back to shelling out to `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`.
855: func resolveRepoRoot(dir string) (string, error) {
856: 	gitPath := filepath.Join(dir, ".git")
857: 	info, err := os.Stat(gitPath)
858: 	if err != nil {
859: 		// .git not found — try git CLI as last resort.
860: 		return resolveRepoRootViaGit(dir)
861: 	}
862: 
863: 	if info.IsDir() {
864: 		// Regular repo: .git is a directory, so dir IS the repo root.
865: 		return dir, nil
866: 	}
867: 
868: 	// Worktree: .git is a file with content "gitdir: <path>"
869: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(gitPath)
870: 	if err != nil {
871: 		return resolveRepoRootViaGit(dir)
872: 	}
873: 	line := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
874: 	if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "gitdir: ") {
875: 		return resolveRepoRootViaGit(dir)
876: 	}
877: 
878: 	// worktreeGitDir is the per-worktree git dir (e.g. /repo/.git/worktrees/name)
879: 	worktreeGitDir := strings.TrimPrefix(line, "gitdir: ")
880: 	if !filepath.IsAbs(worktreeGitDir) {
881: 		worktreeGitDir = filepath.Join(dir, worktreeGitDir)
882: 	}
883: 	worktreeGitDir = filepath.Clean(worktreeGitDir)
884: 
885: 	// commondir contains a relative (or absolute) path back to the main .git dir.
886: 	commondirPath := filepath.Join(worktreeGitDir, "commondir")
887: 	commondirData, err := os.ReadFile(commondirPath)
888: 	if err != nil {
889: 		return resolveRepoRootViaGit(dir)
890: 	}
891: 	commondir := strings.TrimSpace(string(commondirData))
892: 
893: 	var mainGitDir string
894: 	if filepath.IsAbs(commondir) {
895: 		mainGitDir = commondir
896: 	} else {
897: 		mainGitDir = filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(worktreeGitDir, commondir))
898: 	}
899: 
900: 	// The repo root is the parent of the main .git directory.
901: 	return filepath.Dir(mainGitDir), nil
902: }
903: 
904: // resolveRepoRootViaGit shells out to git to find the main repository root.
905: // It uses --git-common-dir (which always points to the main repo's .git) rather
906: // than --show-toplevel (which returns the worktree checkout path in worktrees).
907: func resolveRepoRootViaGit(dir string) (string, error) {
908: 	out, err := exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-parse", "--git-common-dir").Output()
909: 	if err != nil {
910: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("resolve repo root for %s: %w", dir, err)
911: 	}
912: 	gitDir := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
913: 	if !filepath.IsAbs(gitDir) {
914: 		gitDir = filepath.Join(dir, gitDir)
915: 	}
916: 	// --git-common-dir returns the .git directory; repo root is its parent.
917: 	return filepath.Dir(filepath.Clean(gitDir)), nil
918: }

(End of file - total 918 lines)

<content>1: package auditlog
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"database/sql"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"strings"
7: 	"time"
8: 
9: 	_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // register sqlite driver
10: )
11: 
12: const auditSchema = `
13: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_events (
14: 	id             INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
15: 	kind           TEXT    NOT NULL,
16: 	timestamp      TEXT    NOT NULL,
17: 	project        TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
18: 	plan_file      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
19: 	instance_title TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
20: 	agent_type     TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
21: 	wave_number    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
22: 	task_number    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
23: 	message        TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
24: 	detail         TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
25: 	level          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info'
26: );
27: 
28: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_project_ts ON audit_events(project, timestamp DESC);
29: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_plan ON audit_events(plan_file, timestamp DESC);
30: `
31: 
32: const maxQueryLimit = 500
33: 
34: // SQLiteLogger is a Logger backed by a SQLite database.
35: type SQLiteLogger struct {
36: 	db *sql.DB
37: }
38: 
39: // NewSQLiteLogger opens (or creates) a SQLite database at dbPath, runs the
40: // audit_events schema, and returns a ready-to-use logger.
41: // Use ":memory:" for an in-memory database (useful in tests).
42: func NewSQLiteLogger(dbPath string) (*SQLiteLogger, error) {
43: 	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dbPath)
44: 	if err != nil {
45: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("open sqlite db for audit log: %w", err)
46: 	}
47: 
48: 	if _, err := db.Exec(auditSchema); err != nil {
49: 		db.Close()
50: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("run audit log schema: %w", err)
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	return &SQLiteLogger{db: db}, nil
54: }
55: 
56: // Emit inserts an audit event into the database. If the event's Timestamp is
57: // zero, it is set to time.Now(). Emit is synchronous and safe to call from the
58: // bubbletea Update goroutine.
59: func (l *SQLiteLogger) Emit(e Event) {
60: 	if e.Timestamp.IsZero() {
61: 		e.Timestamp = time.Now()
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	const q = `
65: 		INSERT INTO audit_events
66: 			(kind, timestamp, project, plan_file, instance_title, agent_type,
67: 			 wave_number, task_number, message, detail, level)
68: 		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
69: 	`
70: 	level := e.Level
71: 	if level == "" {
72: 		level = "info"
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	_, _ = l.db.Exec(q,
76: 		string(e.Kind),
77: 		auditFormatTime(e.Timestamp),
78: 		e.Project,
79: 		e.TaskFile,
80: 		e.InstanceTitle,
81: 		e.AgentType,
82: 		e.WaveNumber,
83: 		e.TaskNumber,
84: 		e.Message,
85: 		e.Detail,
86: 		level,
87: 	)
88: }
89: 
90: // Query returns events matching the filter, ordered newest-first.
91: // Limit is capped at 500.
92: func (l *SQLiteLogger) Query(f QueryFilter) ([]Event, error) {
93: 	limit := f.Limit
94: 	if limit <= 0 || limit > maxQueryLimit {
95: 		limit = maxQueryLimit
96: 	}
97: 
98: 	var conditions []string
99: 	var args []any
100: 
101: 	if f.Project != "" {
102: 		conditions = append(conditions, "project = ?")
103: 		args = append(args, f.Project)
104: 	}
105: 	if f.TaskFile != "" {
106: 		conditions = append(conditions, "plan_file = ?")
107: 		args = append(args, f.TaskFile)
108: 	}
109: 	if f.InstanceTitle != "" {
110: 		conditions = append(conditions, "instance_title = ?")
111: 		args = append(args, f.InstanceTitle)
112: 	}
113: 	if len(f.Kinds) > 0 {
114: 		placeholders := make([]string, len(f.Kinds))
115: 		for i, k := range f.Kinds {
116: 			placeholders[i] = "?"
117: 			args = append(args, string(k))
118: 		}
119: 		conditions = append(conditions, "kind IN ("+strings.Join(placeholders, ", ")+")")
120: 	}
121: 	if !f.After.IsZero() {
122: 		conditions = append(conditions, "timestamp > ?")
123: 		args = append(args, auditFormatTime(f.After))
124: 	}
125: 	if !f.Before.IsZero() {
126: 		conditions = append(conditions, "timestamp < ?")
127: 		args = append(args, auditFormatTime(f.Before))
128: 	}
129: 
130: 	q := `
131: 		SELECT id, kind, timestamp, project, plan_file, instance_title,
132: 		       agent_type, wave_number, task_number, message, detail, level
133: 		FROM audit_events
134: 	`
135: 	if len(conditions) > 0 {
136: 		q += " WHERE " + strings.Join(conditions, " AND ")
137: 	}
138: 	q += fmt.Sprintf(" ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT %d", limit)
139: 
140: 	rows, err := l.db.Query(q, args...)
141: 	if err != nil {
142: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("query audit events: %w", err)
143: 	}
144: 	defer rows.Close()
145: 
146: 	var events []Event
147: 	for rows.Next() {
148: 		var e Event
149: 		var ts string
150: 		if err := rows.Scan(
151: 			&e.ID,
152: 			(*string)(&e.Kind),
153: 			&ts,
154: 			&e.Project,
155: 			&e.TaskFile,
156: 			&e.InstanceTitle,
157: 			&e.AgentType,
158: 			&e.WaveNumber,
159: 			&e.TaskNumber,
160: 			&e.Message,
161: 			&e.Detail,
162: 			&e.Level,
163: 		); err != nil {
164: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scan audit event: %w", err)
165: 		}
166: 		e.Timestamp = auditParseTime(ts)
167: 		events = append(events, e)
168: 	}
169: 	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
170: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("iterate audit events: %w", err)
171: 	}
172: 	return events, nil
173: }
174: 
175: // Close releases the database connection.
176: func (l *SQLiteLogger) Close() error {
177: 	return l.db.Close()
178: }
179: 
180: // auditFormatTime formats a time.Time as RFC3339Nano for storage.
181: // Zero time returns empty string.
182: func auditFormatTime(t time.Time) string {
183: 	if t.IsZero() {
184: 		return ""
185: 	}
186: 	return t.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)
187: }
188: 
189: // auditParseTime parses an RFC3339Nano string.
190: // Returns zero time on empty or invalid input.
191: func auditParseTime(s string) time.Time {
192: 	if s == "" {
193: 		return time.Time{}
194: 	}
195: 	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s)
196: 	if err != nil {
197: 		return time.Time{}
198: 	}
199: 	return t
200: }

(End of file - total 200 lines)
</content>

22: const ProgramAider = "aider"
23: const ProgramGemini = "gemini"
24: const ProgramOpenCode = "opencode"
25: 
26: // ansiRe strips ANSI escape sequences (SGR, cursor movement, etc.) so that
27: // content hashing is not affected by cursor blink, color resets, or other
28: // terminal control codes that change between captures of an otherwise-idle pane.
29: var ansiRe = regexp.MustCompile(`\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]`)
30: 
31: // TmuxSession represents a managed tmux session.
32: // It implements the Session interface defined in session.go.
33: type TmuxSession struct {
34: 	// Initialized by NewTmuxSession
35: 	//
36: 	// sanitizedName is the kas_-prefixed, whitespace-free tmux session name.
37: 	sanitizedName string
38: 	program       string
39: 	// ptyFactory is used to create a PTY for the tmux session.
40: 	ptyFactory PtyFactory
41: 	// cmdExec is used to execute commands in the tmux session.
42: 	cmdExec cmd.Executor
43: 	// skipPermissions appends --dangerously-skip-permissions to Claude commands.
44: 	skipPermissions bool
45: 	// agentType, when non-empty, appends --agent <type> to the program command.
46: 	agentType string
47: 	// initialPrompt, when non-empty, is baked into the CLI command at Start()
48: 	// using per-program syntax (--prompt for opencode, positional for claude).
49: 	initialPrompt string
50: 	// taskNumber, waveNumber, peerCount are set for wave task instances.
51: 	// When non-zero, they are prepended as KASMOS_TASK, KASMOS_WAVE, KASMOS_PEERS
52: 	// env vars to the program command string.
53: 	taskNumber int
54: 	waveNumber int
55: 	peerCount  int
56: 	// ProgressFunc is called with (stage, description) during Start() to report progress.
57: 	ProgressFunc func(stage int, desc string)
58: 	// promptFile is the path to a temporary file containing the initial prompt.
59: 	// Set during Start() when the prompt exceeds maxInlinePromptLen. Cleaned up by Close().
60: 	promptFile string
61: 	// sessionTitle is the desired human-readable title for the opencode session.
62: 	// When non-empty and titleFunc is set, the title is applied after the session
63: 	// reports ready (after "Ask anything" is detected).
64: 	sessionTitle string
65: 	// titleFunc is a callback that performs the actual DB title update for opencode sessions.
66: 	// It is injected by the instance layer to avoid a direct dependency from tmux → opencodesession.
67: 	// Called as a goroutine (best-effort, non-blocking) after session startup.
68: 	titleFunc func(workDir string, beforeStart time.Time, title string)
69: 
70: 	// Initialized by Start or Restore
71: 	//
72: 	// ptmx is a PTY running the tmux attach command. This can be resized to change the
73: 	// stdout dimensions of the tmux pane. On detach, we close it and set a new one.
74: 	// This should never be nil after a successful Start or Restore.
75: 	ptmx *os.File
76: 	// monitor monitors the tmux pane content and sends signals to the UI when its status changes.
77: 	monitor *StatusMonitor
78: 
79: 	// Initialized by Attach; deinitialized by Detach.
80: 	//
81: 	// attachCh is closed at the very end of detaching. Used to signal callers.
82: 	attachCh chan struct{}
83: 	// ctx, cancel, wg manage goroutines launched by Attach.
84: 	ctx    context.Context
85: 	cancel func()
86: 	wg     *sync.WaitGroup
87: 	// outerMouseWasEnabled is set when Attach() disables mouse on the outer tmux
88: 	// session so Detach() can restore it.

92: // TmuxPrefix is the prefix added to all kas-managed tmux session names.
93: const TmuxPrefix = "kas_"
94: 
95: var whiteSpaceRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`\s+`)
96: 
97: // cleanupSessionsRe matches current kas_ sessions and legacy klique_/hivemind_ sessions.
98: var cleanupSessionsRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?:kas_|klique_|hivemind_).*:`)
99: 
100: // toKasTmuxName converts a human-readable name to a kas_-prefixed tmux session name.
101: // Whitespace is removed and dots are replaced with underscores (tmux does this natively).
102: func toKasTmuxName(str string) string {
103: 	str = whiteSpaceRegex.ReplaceAllString(str, "")
104: 	str = strings.ReplaceAll(str, ".", "_") // tmux replaces all . with _
105: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s%s", TmuxPrefix, str)
106: }
107: 
108: // ToKasTmuxNamePublic is the exported version of toKasTmuxName for use by the app layer.
109: func ToKasTmuxNamePublic(name string) string {
110: 	return toKasTmuxName(name)
111: }
112: 
113: // NewTmuxSession creates a new TmuxSession with the given name and program.
114: func NewTmuxSession(name string, program string, skipPermissions bool) *TmuxSession {
115: 	return newTmuxSession(name, program, skipPermissions, MakePtyFactory(), cmd.MakeExecutor())
116: }
117: 
118: // NewTmuxSessionWithDeps creates a new TmuxSession with provided dependencies for testing.
119: func NewTmuxSessionWithDeps(name string, program string, skipPermissions bool, ptyFactory PtyFactory, cmdExec cmd.Executor) *TmuxSession {
120: 	return newTmuxSession(name, program, skipPermissions, ptyFactory, cmdExec)
121: }
122: 
123: func newTmuxSession(name string, program string, skipPermissions bool, ptyFactory PtyFactory, cmdExec cmd.Executor) *TmuxSession {
124: 	return &TmuxSession{
125: 		sanitizedName:   toKasTmuxName(name),
126: 		program:         program,
127: 		skipPermissions: skipPermissions,
128: 		ptyFactory:      ptyFactory,
129: 		cmdExec:         cmdExec,
130: 	}
131: }
132: 
133: // NewReset creates a fresh TmuxSession preserving the ptyFactory and cmdExec
134: // from the receiver. Used by Instance.Restart() to avoid replacing injected
135: // test dependencies with real implementations.
136: func (t *TmuxSession) NewReset(name string, program string, skipPermissions bool) *TmuxSession {
137: 	return newTmuxSession(name, program, skipPermissions, t.ptyFactory, t.cmdExec)
138: }
139: 
140: // NewTmuxSessionFromExisting creates a TmuxSession that wraps an already-running
141: // tmux session identified by its raw sanitized name. Used for adopting orphans.
142: func NewTmuxSessionFromExisting(sanitizedName string, program string, skipPermissions bool) *TmuxSession {
143: 	return &TmuxSession{
144: 		sanitizedName:   sanitizedName,
145: 		program:         program,

147: 		ptyFactory:      MakePtyFactory(),

155: 	t.agentType = strings.TrimSpace(agentType)
156: }

158: // SetInitialPrompt sets the initial prompt to bake into the CLI command at launch.
159: // Supported programs: opencode (--prompt), claude (positional arg).
160: // For unsupported programs the prompt is ignored; callers should keep
161: // QueuedPrompt set so the send-keys fallback fires.
162: func (t *TmuxSession) SetInitialPrompt(prompt string) {
163: 	t.initialPrompt = prompt
164: }
165: 
166: // SetTaskEnv sets the task identity env vars for parallel wave execution.
167: // When set, KASMOS_TASK, KASMOS_WAVE, and KASMOS_PEERS are prepended to the
168: // program command string at Start() time.
169: func (t *TmuxSession) SetTaskEnv(taskNumber, waveNumber, peerCount int) {
170: 	t.taskNumber = taskNumber
171: 	t.waveNumber = waveNumber
172: 	t.peerCount = peerCount

175: // SetSessionTitle sets the desired title for the opencode session.
176: // When non-empty and a titleFunc is also set, the title is applied after
177: // the session reports ready. The "kas: " prefix is expected to already be
178: // included by the caller (e.g. from BuildTitle in the opencodesession package).
179: func (t *TmuxSession) SetSessionTitle(title string) {
180: 	t.sessionTitle = title
181: }
182: 
183: // SetTitleFunc sets the callback used to apply the session title after startup.
184: // The callback receives the work directory, a timestamp taken just before the
185: // tmux session was created, and the desired title string. It runs in a goroutine
186: // (fire-and-forget) so title setting never blocks or delays session startup.
187: func (t *TmuxSession) SetTitleFunc(fn func(workDir string, beforeStart time.Time, title string)) {
188: 	t.titleFunc = fn
189: }
190: 
191: // reportProgress calls ProgressFunc if set.
192: func (t *TmuxSession) reportProgress(stage int, desc string) {
193: 	if t.ProgressFunc != nil {
194: 		t.ProgressFunc(stage, desc)
195: 	}
196: }
197: 
198: // isClaudeProgram returns true if the program string refers to Claude Code.
199: func isClaudeProgram(program string) bool {
200: 	return strings.HasSuffix(program, ProgramClaude)
201: }
202: 
203: // isAiderProgram returns true if the program string refers to Aider.
204: // Kept for compatibility with existing tmux_io.go references during the clean-room rewrite.
205: // Will be removed when aider support is fully dropped.
206: func isAiderProgram(program string) bool {
207: 	return strings.HasPrefix(program, ProgramAider)
208: }
209: 
210: // isGeminiProgram returns true if the program string refers to Gemini.
211: // Kept for compatibility with existing tmux_io.go references during the clean-room rewrite.
212: // Will be removed when gemini support is fully dropped.
213: func isGeminiProgram(program string) bool {
214: 	return strings.HasPrefix(program, ProgramGemini)
215: }
216: 
217: // isOpenCodeProgram returns true if the program string refers to OpenCode.
218: func isOpenCodeProgram(program string) bool {
219: 	return strings.HasSuffix(program, ProgramOpenCode)
220: }
221: 
222: // Start creates and starts a new tmux session, then attaches to it.
223: // program is the command to run in the session. workDir is the git worktree directory.
224: func (t *TmuxSession) Start(workDir string) error {
225: 	// Reattach to a surviving session from a previous crash/interrupt
226: 	// instead of killing it — the agent may still be running.
227: 	if t.DoesSessionExist() {
228: 		return t.Restore()
229: 	}
230: 
231: 	// Append --dangerously-skip-permissions for Claude programs if enabled.
232: 	program := t.program
233: 	if t.skipPermissions && isClaudeProgram(program) {
234: 		program = program + " --dangerously-skip-permissions"
235: 	}
236: 	if t.agentType != "" && !strings.Contains(program, "--agent") {
237: 		program = program + " --agent " + t.agentType
238: 	}
239: 	if t.initialPrompt != "" {
240: 		switch {
241: 		case isOpenCodeProgram(t.program):
242: 			program = program + " --prompt " + t.promptArgOpenCode(workDir)
243: 		case isClaudeProgram(t.program):
244: 			program = program + " " + t.promptArgClaude(workDir)
245: 		}
246: 		// aider/gemini: no CLI prompt support — callers keep QueuedPrompt
247: 		// set so the send-keys fallback fires from the app tick handler.
248: 	}
249: 
250: 	// Append --print-logs and redirect stderr to a per-session log file so
251: 	// kasmos-spawned opencode agents always have debug logs available.
252: 	if isOpenCodeProgram(t.program) {
253: 		logDir := filepath.Join(workDir, promptDir, "logs")
254: 		if err := os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0o755); err == nil {
255: 			logFile := filepath.Join(logDir, t.sanitizedName+".log")
256: 			program = program + " --print-logs 2>>" + shellEscapeSingleQuote(logFile)
257: 		}
258: 	}
259: 
260: 	// Prepend KASMOS_MANAGED=1 so the agent process sees it from startup.
261: 	program = "KASMOS_MANAGED=1 " + program
262: 
263: 	// Prepend task identity env vars for parallel wave execution.
264: 	if t.taskNumber > 0 {
265: 		program = fmt.Sprintf("KASMOS_TASK=%d KASMOS_WAVE=%d KASMOS_PEERS=%d %s",
266: 			t.taskNumber, t.waveNumber, t.peerCount, program)
267: 	}
268: 
269: 	t.reportProgress(1, "Creating tmux session...")
270: 
271: 	// Record the timestamp immediately before launching the tmux session.
272: 	// This is passed to titleFunc so it can find the opencode DB session
273: 	// that was created at or after this moment.
274: 	beforeStart := time.Now()
275: 
276: 	// Create a new detached tmux session and start the program in it.
277: 	cmd := exec.Command("tmux", "new-session", "-d", "-s", t.sanitizedName, "-c", workDir, program)
278: 
279: 	ptmx, err := t.ptyFactory.Start(cmd)
280: 	if err != nil {
281: 		// Cleanup any partially created session if any exists.
282: 		if t.DoesSessionExist() {
283: 			cleanupCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "kill-session", "-t", t.sanitizedName)
284: 			if cleanupErr := t.cmdExec.Run(cleanupCmd); cleanupErr != nil {
285: 				err = fmt.Errorf("%v (cleanup error: %v)", err, cleanupErr)
286: 			}
287: 		}
288: 		return fmt.Errorf("error starting tmux session: %w", err)
289: 	}
290: 
291: 	t.reportProgress(2, "Waiting for session to start...")
292: 
293: 	// Poll for session existence with exponential backoff.
294: 	timeout := time.After(2 * time.Second)
295: 	sleepDuration := 5 * time.Millisecond
296: 	for !t.DoesSessionExist() {
297: 		select {
298: 		case <-timeout:
299: 			if cleanupErr := t.Close(); cleanupErr != nil {
300: 				err = fmt.Errorf("%v (cleanup error: %v)", err, cleanupErr)
301: 			}
302: 			return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for tmux session %s: %v", t.sanitizedName, err)
303: 		default:
304: 			time.Sleep(sleepDuration)
305: 			// Exponential backoff up to 50ms max.
306: 			if sleepDuration < 50*time.Millisecond {
307: 				sleepDuration *= 2
308: 			}
309: 		}
310: 	}
311: 	ptmx.Close()
312: 
313: 	// Set history limit to enable scrollback (default is 2000, we use 10000 for more history).
314: 	historyCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "set-option", "-t", t.sanitizedName, "history-limit", "10000")
315: 	if err := t.cmdExec.Run(historyCmd); err != nil {
316: 		log.InfoLog.Printf("Warning: failed to set history-limit for session %s: %v", t.sanitizedName, err)
317: 	}
318: 
319: 	// Hide the tmux status bar — the kasmos TUI provides its own chrome.
320: 	statusCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "set-option", "-t", t.sanitizedName, "status", "off")
321: 	if err := t.cmdExec.Run(statusCmd); err != nil {
322: 		log.InfoLog.Printf("Warning: failed to hide status bar for session %s: %v", t.sanitizedName, err)
323: 	}
324: 
325: 	// Set escape-time to 0 so ESC is forwarded immediately to the inner program.
326: 	// Without this, tmux waits its default 500ms to disambiguate ESC from escape
327: 	// sequences, making ESC feel broken in focus/interactive mode.
328: 	escapeTimeCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "set-option", "-t", t.sanitizedName, "escape-time", "0")
329: 	if err := t.cmdExec.Run(escapeTimeCmd); err != nil {
330: 		log.InfoLog.Printf("Warning: failed to set escape-time for session %s: %v", t.sanitizedName, err)
331: 	}
332: 
333: 	// Inject KASMOS_MANAGED=1 so agents can detect they're running under kasmos orchestration.
334: 	envCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "set-environment", "-t", t.sanitizedName, "KASMOS_MANAGED", "1")
335: 	if err := t.cmdExec.Run(envCmd); err != nil {
336: 		log.InfoLog.Printf("Warning: failed to set KASMOS_MANAGED env for session %s: %v", t.sanitizedName, err)
337: 	}
338: 
339: 	t.reportProgress(3, "Configuring session...")
340: 
341: 	err = t.Restore()
342: 	if err != nil {
343: 		if cleanupErr := t.Close(); cleanupErr != nil {
344: 			err = fmt.Errorf("%v (cleanup error: %v)", err, cleanupErr)
345: 		}
346: 		return fmt.Errorf("error restoring tmux session: %w", err)
347: 	}
348: 
349: 	if isClaudeProgram(t.program) || isAiderProgram(t.program) || isGeminiProgram(t.program) || isOpenCodeProgram(t.program) {
350: 		t.reportProgress(4, "Waiting for program to start...")
351: 
352: 		var searchString string
353: 		var tapFunc func() error // nil means no key tap needed (e.g. opencode)
354: 		maxWaitTime := 30 * time.Second
355: 
356: 		switch {
357: 		case isClaudeProgram(t.program):
358: 			searchString = "Do you trust the files in this folder?"
359: 			tapFunc = t.TapEnter
360: 		case isOpenCodeProgram(t.program):
361: 			// opencode shows its input placeholder once the TUI is ready; no tap needed.
362: 			searchString = "Ask anything"
363: 			tapFunc = nil
364: 		default: // aider / gemini
365: 			searchString = "Open documentation url for more info"
366: 			tapFunc = t.TapDAndEnter
367: 			maxWaitTime = 45 * time.Second
368: 		}
369: 
370: 		// Poll with exponential backoff until the ready string appears or we time out.
371: 		startTime := time.Now()
372: 		sleepDuration := 100 * time.Millisecond
373: 
374: 		for time.Since(startTime) < maxWaitTime {
375: 			time.Sleep(sleepDuration)
376: 			content, err := t.CapturePaneContent()
377: 			if err == nil && strings.Contains(content, searchString) {
378: 				if tapFunc != nil {
379: 					if err := tapFunc(); err != nil {
380: 						log.ErrorLog.Printf("could not tap enter on trust screen: %v", err)
381: 					}
382: 				}
383: 				break
384: 			}
385: 
386: 			// Exponential backoff with cap at 1 second.
387: 			sleepDuration = time.Duration(float64(sleepDuration) * 1.2)
388: 			if sleepDuration > time.Second {
389: 				sleepDuration = time.Second
390: 			}
391: 		}
392: 
393: 		// After the session is ready, set the opencode session title (best-effort, non-blocking).
394: 		// Only fires for opencode programs with a title and callback configured.
395: 		if isOpenCodeProgram(t.program) && t.sessionTitle != "" && t.titleFunc != nil {
396: 			go t.titleFunc(workDir, beforeStart, t.sessionTitle)
397: 		}
398: 	}
399: 	return nil
400: }
401: 
402: // Restore attaches to an existing session and restores the window size.
403: func (t *TmuxSession) Restore() error {
404: 	ptmx, err := t.ptyFactory.Start(exec.Command("tmux", "attach-session", "-t", t.sanitizedName))
405: 	if err != nil {
406: 		return fmt.Errorf("error opening PTY: %w", err)
407: 	}
408: 	t.ptmx = ptmx
409: 	t.monitor = NewStatusMonitor()
410: 	// Idempotently hide the status bar — also covers sessions restored from crash
411: 	// that were created before this option was set.
412: 	statusCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "set-option", "-t", t.sanitizedName, "status", "off")
413: 	if err := t.cmdExec.Run(statusCmd); err != nil {
414: 		log.InfoLog.Printf("Warning: failed to hide status bar for restored session %s: %v", t.sanitizedName, err)
415: 	}
416: 	// Idempotently set escape-time to 0 for immediate ESC forwarding.
417: 	escapeTimeCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "set-option", "-t", t.sanitizedName, "escape-time", "0")
418: 	if err := t.cmdExec.Run(escapeTimeCmd); err != nil {
419: 		log.InfoLog.Printf("Warning: failed to set escape-time for restored session %s: %v", t.sanitizedName, err)
420: 	}
421: 	return nil
422: }
423: 
424: // outerTmuxSession returns the name of the enclosing tmux session (the one
425: // running kasmos), or "" if we are not inside tmux.
426: func outerTmuxSession() string {
427: 	if os.Getenv("TMUX") == "" {
428: 		return ""
429: 	}
430: 	out, err := exec.Command("tmux", "display-message", "-p", "#{session_name}").Output()
431: 	if err != nil {
432: 		return ""
433: 	}
434: 	return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
435: }
436: 
437: // outerMouseEnabled reports whether mouse is currently on in the given tmux session.
438: func outerMouseEnabled(session string) bool {
439: 	out, err := exec.Command("tmux", "show-options", "-t", session, "mouse").Output()
440: 	if err != nil {
441: 		return false
442: 	}
443: 	return strings.Contains(string(out), "on")
444: }
445: 
446: // Close terminates the tmux session and cleans up resources.
447: func (t *TmuxSession) Close() error {
448: 	var errs []error
449: 
450: 	if t.ptmx != nil {
451: 		if err := t.ptmx.Close(); err != nil {
452: 			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("error closing PTY: %w", err))
453: 		}
454: 		t.ptmx = nil
455: 	}
456: 
457: 	cmd := exec.Command("tmux", "kill-session", "-t", t.sanitizedName)
458: 	if err := t.cmdExec.Run(cmd); err != nil {
459: 		errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("error killing tmux session: %w", err))
460: 	}
461: 
462: 	if t.promptFile != "" {
463: 		os.Remove(t.promptFile)
464: 		t.promptFile = ""
465: 	}
466: 
467: 	return errors.Join(errs...)
468: }
469: 
470: // DoesSessionExist returns true if the tmux session is currently running.
471: func (t *TmuxSession) DoesSessionExist() bool {
472: 	// Using "-t name" does a prefix match, which is wrong. `-t=` does an exact match.
473: 	existsCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "has-session", fmt.Sprintf("-t=%s", t.sanitizedName))
474: 	return t.cmdExec.Run(existsCmd) == nil
475: }
476: 
477: // CleanupSessions kills all tmux sessions that start with the kas prefix.
478: // Also cleans up legacy "hivemind_" and "klique_" sessions from before the rename.
479: func CleanupSessions(cmdExec cmd.Executor) error {
480: 	// First try to list sessions.
481: 	cmd := exec.Command("tmux", "ls")
482: 	output, err := cmdExec.Output(cmd)
483: 
484: 	// If there's an error and it's because no server is running, that's fine.
485: 	// Exit code 1 typically means no sessions exist.
486: 	if err != nil {
487: 		if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok && exitErr.ExitCode() == 1 {
488: 			return nil // No sessions to clean up.
489: 		}
490: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to list tmux sessions: %v", err)
491: 	}
492: 
493: 	matches := cleanupSessionsRe.FindAllString(string(output), -1)
494: 	for i, match := range matches {
495: 		matches[i] = match[:strings.Index(match, ":")]
496: 	}
497: 
498: 	for _, match := range matches {
499: 		log.InfoLog.Printf("cleaning up session: %s", match)
500: 		if err := cmdExec.Run(exec.Command("tmux", "kill-session", "-t", match)); err != nil {
501: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to kill tmux session %s: %v", match, err)
502: 		}
503: 	}
504: 	return nil
505: }
506: 
507: // OrphanSession represents a kas_ tmux session not tracked by any kasmos Instance.
508: type OrphanSession struct {
509: 	Name     string    // raw tmux session name, e.g. "kas_auth-refactor-implement"
510: 	Title    string    // human name with "kas_" prefix stripped
511: 	Created  time.Time // session creation time
512: 	Windows  int       // window count
513: 	Attached bool      // whether another client is attached
514: 	Width    int       // pane columns
515: 	Height   int       // pane rows
516: }
517: 
518: // DiscoverOrphans lists kas_-prefixed tmux sessions that are NOT in knownNames.
519: // knownNames should contain the sanitized tmux names of all current Instances.
520: func DiscoverOrphans(cmdExec cmd.Executor, knownNames []string) ([]OrphanSession, error) {
521: 	lsCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "ls", "-F",
522: 		"#{session_name}|#{session_created}|#{session_windows}|#{session_attached}|#{window_width}|#{window_height}")
523: 	output, err := cmdExec.Output(lsCmd)
524: 	if err != nil {
525: 		if _, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
526: 			return nil, nil // no tmux server running or no sessions
527: 		}
528: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to list tmux sessions: %w", err)
529: 	}
530: 
531: 	known := make(map[string]bool, len(knownNames))
532: 	for _, n := range knownNames {
533: 		known[n] = true
534: 	}
535: 
536: 	var orphans []OrphanSession
537: 	for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(output)), "\n") {
538: 		if line == "" {
539: 			continue
540: 		}
541: 		parts := strings.SplitN(line, "|", 6)
542: 		if len(parts) < 6 {
543: 			continue
544: 		}
545: 		name := parts[0]
546: 		if !strings.HasPrefix(name, TmuxPrefix) {
547: 			continue
548: 		}
549: 		if known[name] {
550: 			continue
551: 		}
552: 
553: 		var created time.Time
554: 		if epoch, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64); err == nil {
555: 			created = time.Unix(epoch, 0)
556: 		}
557: 		windows, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[2])
558: 		attached := parts[3] != "0"
559: 		width, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[4])
560: 		height, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[5])
561: 
562: 		title := strings.TrimPrefix(name, TmuxPrefix)
563: 		orphans = append(orphans, OrphanSession{
564: 			Name:     name,
565: 			Title:    title,
566: 			Created:  created,
567: 			Windows:  windows,
568: 			Attached: attached,
569: 			Width:    width,
570: 			Height:   height,
571: 		})
572: 	}
573: 	return orphans, nil
574: }
575: 
576: // SessionInfo represents any kas_ tmux session (managed or orphaned).
577: type SessionInfo struct {
578: 	Name     string    // raw tmux session name, e.g. "kas_auth-refactor-implement"
579: 	Title    string    // human name with "kas_" prefix stripped
580: 	Created  time.Time // session creation time
581: 	Windows  int       // window count
582: 	Attached bool      // whether another client is attached
583: 	Width    int       // pane columns
584: 	Height   int       // pane rows
585: 	Managed  bool      // true if matched a known instance name
586: }
587: 
588: // DiscoverAll lists all kas_-prefixed tmux sessions, marking each as Managed
589: // if its name appears in knownNames. knownNames should contain the sanitized
590: // tmux names of all current Instances (e.g. from ToKasTmuxNamePublic).
591: func DiscoverAll(cmdExec cmd.Executor, knownNames []string) ([]SessionInfo, error) {
592: 	lsCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "ls", "-F",
593: 		"#{session_name}|#{session_created}|#{session_windows}|#{session_attached}|#{window_width}|#{window_height}")
594: 	output, err := cmdExec.Output(lsCmd)
595: 	if err != nil {
596: 		if _, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
597: 			return nil, nil
598: 		}
599: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to list tmux sessions: %w", err)
600: 	}
601: 
602: 	known := make(map[string]bool, len(knownNames))
603: 	for _, n := range knownNames {
604: 		known[n] = true
605: 	}
606: 
607: 	var sessions []SessionInfo
608: 	for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(output)), "\n") {
609: 		if line == "" {
610: 			continue
611: 		}
612: 		parts := strings.SplitN(line, "|", 6)
613: 		if len(parts) < 6 {
614: 			continue
615: 		}
616: 		name := parts[0]
617: 		if !strings.HasPrefix(name, TmuxPrefix) {
618: 			continue
619: 		}
620: 
621: 		var created time.Time
622: 		if epoch, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64); err == nil {
623: 			created = time.Unix(epoch, 0)
624: 		}
625: 		windows, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[2])
626: 		attached := parts[3] != "0"
627: 		width, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[4])
628: 		height, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[5])
629: 
630: 		sessions = append(sessions, SessionInfo{
631: 			Name:     name,
632: 			Title:    strings.TrimPrefix(name, TmuxPrefix),
633: 			Created:  created,
634: 			Windows:  windows,
635: 			Attached: attached,
636: 			Width:    width,
637: 			Height:   height,
638: 			Managed:  known[name],
639: 		})
640: 	}
641: 	return sessions, nil
642: }
643: 
644: // CountKasSessions returns the number of kas_-prefixed tmux sessions.
645: // Returns 0 if no tmux server is running or on any error.
646: func CountKasSessions(cmdExec cmd.Executor) int {
647: 	lsCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "ls")
648: 	output, err := cmdExec.Output(lsCmd)
649: 	if err != nil {
650: 		return 0
651: 	}
652: 	count := 0
653: 	for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(output)), "\n") {
654: 		if strings.HasPrefix(line, TmuxPrefix) {
655: 			count++
656: 		}
657: 	}
658: 	return count
659: }

11: // TmuxBrowserItem holds metadata for a single tmux session (managed or orphaned).

14: 	Title     string
15: 	Created   time.Time
16: 	Windows   int
17: 	Attached  bool

23: 	Status    string // "running"/"ready"/"loading"/"paused" (managed only)

26: // TmuxBrowserOverlay shows orphaned tmux sessions with kill/adopt/attach actions.

35: // NewTmuxBrowserOverlay creates a browser overlay from discovered orphan sessions.
36: func NewTmuxBrowserOverlay(items []TmuxBrowserItem) *TmuxBrowserOverlay {
37: 	b := &TmuxBrowserOverlay{
38: 		sessions: items,
39: 		width:    56,
40: 	}
41: 	b.applyFilter()
42: 	return b

45: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) applyFilter() {
46: 	b.filtered = nil
47: 	query := strings.ToLower(b.searchQuery)
48: 	for i, item := range b.sessions {
49: 		if query == "" || strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(item.Title), query) {
50: 			b.filtered = append(b.filtered, i)
51: 		}
52: 	}
53: 	if b.selectedIdx >= len(b.filtered) {
54: 		b.selectedIdx = len(b.filtered) - 1
55: 	}
56: 	if b.selectedIdx < 0 {

58: 	}

60: 
61: // SelectedItem returns the currently highlighted session, or a zero value if empty.
62: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) SelectedItem() TmuxBrowserItem {
63: 	if len(b.filtered) == 0 || b.selectedIdx >= len(b.filtered) {
64: 		return TmuxBrowserItem{}
65: 	}
66: 	return b.sessions[b.filtered[b.selectedIdx]]
67: }
68: 
69: // RemoveSelected removes the currently selected item from the list.
70: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) RemoveSelected() {
71: 	if len(b.filtered) == 0 || b.selectedIdx >= len(b.filtered) {
72: 		return
73: 	}
74: 	idx := b.filtered[b.selectedIdx]
75: 	b.sessions = append(b.sessions[:idx], b.sessions[idx+1:]...)
76: 	b.applyFilter()
77: }
78: 
79: // IsEmpty returns true if there are no sessions to display.
80: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) IsEmpty() bool {
81: 	return len(b.sessions) == 0
82: }
83: 
84: // render draws the browser overlay.
85: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) render() string {
86: 	st := DefaultStyles()
87: 	var s strings.Builder
88: 
89: 	s.WriteString(st.Title.Render("tmux sessions"))
90: 	s.WriteString("\n")
91: 
92: 	// Search bar
93: 	// lipgloss v2: Width() = total outer width. FloatingBorder frame = 6.
94: 	innerWidth := b.width - 6
95: 	if innerWidth < 10 {
96: 		innerWidth = 10
97: 	}
98: 	searchText := b.searchQuery
99: 	if searchText == "" {
100: 		searchText = st.Muted.Render("\uf002 type to filter...")
101: 	}
102: 	s.WriteString(st.SearchBar.Width(innerWidth).Render(searchText))
103: 	s.WriteString("\n")
104: 
105: 	// Items
106: 	if len(b.filtered) == 0 {
107: 		s.WriteString(st.Muted.Render("  no sessions"))
108: 		s.WriteString("\n")
109: 	} else {
110: 		for i, idx := range b.filtered {
111: 			item := b.sessions[idx]
112: 			age := relativeTime(item.Created)
113: 			dims := fmt.Sprintf("%d×%d", item.Width, item.Height)
114: 
115: 			attachedIndicator := "  "
116: 			if item.Attached {
117: 				attachedIndicator = "● "
118: 			}
119: 
120: 			// Badge for managed items
121: 			badge := ""
122: 			if item.Managed {
123: 				badgeText := "managed"
124: 				if item.AgentType != "" {
125: 					badgeText = item.AgentType
126: 				}
127: 				badge = st.Muted.Render(" [" + badgeText + "]")
128: 			}
129: 
130: 			label := fmt.Sprintf("%-28s %8s %s%s",
131: 				truncateStr(item.Title, 28), age, attachedIndicator, dims) + badge
132: 
133: 			if i == b.selectedIdx {
134: 				s.WriteString(st.SelectedItem.Width(innerWidth).Render("▸ " + label))
135: 			} else {
136: 				s.WriteString(st.Item.Width(innerWidth).Render("  " + label))
137: 			}
138: 			s.WriteString("\n")
139: 		}
140: 	}
141: 
142: 	hint := "↑↓ navigate · k kill · o attach · esc close"
143: 	if len(b.filtered) > 0 && !b.SelectedItem().Managed {
144: 		hint = "↑↓ navigate · k kill · a adopt · o attach · esc close"
145: 	}
146: 	s.WriteString(st.Hint.Render(hint))
147: 
148: 	return st.FloatingBorder.Width(b.width).Render(s.String())
149: }
150: 
151: // SetSize updates the overlay width.
152: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) SetSize(width, height int) {
153: 	b.width = width
154: }
155: 
156: // HandleKey implements Overlay. Processes a key event and returns a Result.
157: //
158: // "kill" returns Result{Action: "kill"} without Dismissed so the browser stays
159: // open and the user can kill multiple sessions. The app layer must handle
160: // non-dismissed action results. "adopt" and "attach" do dismiss the overlay.
161: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) HandleKey(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) Result {
162: 	switch msg.Code {
163: 	case tea.KeyEscape:
164: 		if b.searchQuery != "" {
165: 			b.searchQuery = ""
166: 			b.applyFilter()
167: 			return Result{}
168: 		}
169: 		return Result{Dismissed: true}
170: 	case tea.KeyEnter:
171: 		if len(b.filtered) > 0 {
172: 			return Result{Dismissed: true, Action: "attach"}
173: 		}
174: 		return Result{}
175: 	case tea.KeyUp:
176: 		if b.selectedIdx > 0 {
177: 			b.selectedIdx--
178: 		}
179: 		return Result{}
180: 	case tea.KeyDown:
181: 		if b.selectedIdx < len(b.filtered)-1 {
182: 			b.selectedIdx++
183: 		}
184: 		return Result{}
185: 	case tea.KeyBackspace:
186: 		if len(b.searchQuery) > 0 {
187: 			runes := []rune(b.searchQuery)
188: 			b.searchQuery = string(runes[:len(runes)-1])
189: 			b.applyFilter()
190: 		}
191: 		return Result{}
192: 	default:
193: 		if len(msg.Text) > 0 {
194: 			r := msg.Text
195: 			// Action keys only fire when search is empty
196: 			if b.searchQuery == "" {
197: 				switch r {
198: 				case "k":
199: 					if len(b.filtered) > 0 {
200: 						// Do NOT dismiss — browser stays open for multi-kill workflow.
201: 						// The app layer handles the action via the non-dismissed path.
202: 						return Result{Action: "kill"}
203: 					}
204: 					return Result{}
205: 				case "a":
206: 					if len(b.filtered) > 0 && !b.SelectedItem().Managed {
207: 						return Result{Dismissed: true, Action: "adopt"}
208: 					}
209: 					return Result{}
210: 				case "o":
211: 					if len(b.filtered) > 0 {
212: 						return Result{Dismissed: true, Action: "attach"}
213: 					}
214: 					return Result{}
215: 				}
216: 			}
217: 			// All other runes type into search
218: 			b.searchQuery += r
219: 			b.applyFilter()
220: 			return Result{}
221: 		}
222: 	}
223: 	return Result{}
224: }
225: 
226: // View implements Overlay. Returns the rendered overlay string.
227: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) View() string {
228: 	return b.render()
229: }
230: 
231: // truncateStr truncates s to maxLen runes, appending "…" if truncated.
232: func truncateStr(s string, maxLen int) string {
233: 	runes := []rune(s)
234: 	if len(runes) <= maxLen {
235: 		return s
236: 	}
237: 	return string(runes[:maxLen-1]) + "…"
238: }
239: 
240: // relativeTime returns a human-readable relative time string.
241: func relativeTime(t time.Time) string {
242: 	if t.IsZero() {
243: 		return ""
244: 	}
245: 	d := time.Since(t)
246: 	switch {
247: 	case d < time.Minute:
248: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%ds ago", int(d.Seconds()))
249: 	case d < time.Hour:
250: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%dm ago", int(d.Minutes()))
251: 	case d < 24*time.Hour:
252: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%dh ago", int(d.Hours()))
253: 	default:
254: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%dd ago", int(d.Hours()/24))
255: 	}
256: }

1947: // refreshAuditPane queries the audit logger and updates the audit pane display.
1948: // Shows a global activity feed — not filtered by sidebar selection.
1949: func (m *home) refreshAuditPane() {
1950: 	if m.auditPane == nil || m.auditLogger == nil {
1951: 		return
1952: 	}
1953: 
1954: 	filter := auditlog.QueryFilter{
1955: 		Project: m.taskStoreProject,
1956: 		Limit:   200,
1957: 	}
1958: 
1959: 	events, err := m.auditLogger.Query(filter)
1960: 	if err != nil {
1961: 		return
1962: 	}
1963: 
1964: 	displays := make([]ui.AuditEventDisplay, 0, len(events))
1965: 	for _, e := range events {
1966: 		icon, color := ui.EventKindIcon(string(e.Kind))
1967: 		timeStr := e.Timestamp.Local().Format("15:04")
1968: 		msg := e.Message
1969: 		// Prepend [plan-name] when the event has a plan context and the message
1970: 		// doesn't already embed the plan name (some messages include it inline).
1971: 		if e.TaskFile != "" {
1972: 			label := taskstate.DisplayName(e.TaskFile)
1973: 			if !strings.Contains(msg, label) {
1974: 				msg = "[" + label + "] " + msg
1975: 			}
1976: 		}
1977: 		displays = append(displays, ui.AuditEventDisplay{
1978: 			Time:    timeStr,
1979: 			Kind:    string(e.Kind),
1980: 			Icon:    icon,
1981: 			Message: msg,
1982: 			Color:   color,
1983: 			Level:   e.Level,
1984: 		})
1985: 	}
1986: 
1987: 	// Coalesce consecutive stopped+started pairs (newest-first order) with the
1988: 	// same HH:MM timestamp into a single "kasmos restarted" event.
1989: 	displays = coalesceRestarts(displays)
1990: 
1991: 	m.auditPane.SetEvents(displays)
1992: 
1993: 	// Push updated audit view into the nav panel.
1994: 	if m.nav != nil && m.auditPane.Visible() {
1995: 		m.nav.SetAuditView(m.auditPane.String(), m.auditPane.ContentLines())
1996: 	}
1997: }

8: // NewStoreFromConfig creates a Store from a plan store URL and project name.
9: // If storeURL is empty, it returns (nil, nil) — the caller should fall
10: // back to legacy plan-state.json behavior.
11: // The returned store uses lazy connection: the URL is validated syntactically
12: // but no network connection is made until the first operation (or Ping).
13: func NewStoreFromConfig(storeURL, project string) (Store, error) {
14: 	if storeURL == "" {
15: 		return nil, nil // no remote store configured
16: 	}
17: 	return NewHTTPStore(storeURL, project), nil
18: }
19: 
20: // ResolvedDBPath returns the filesystem path that the factory would use for a
21: // local SQLite taskstore. It reads the XDG-compliant config directory
22: // (~/.config/kasmos/) and appends "taskstore.db". This path is shared with
23: // the auditlog SQLiteLogger so both can coexist in the same database file
24: // (each using a separate table).
25: func ResolvedDBPath() string {
26: 	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
27: 	if err != nil {
28: 		return filepath.Join(".", "taskstore.db")
29: 	}

<content>1: package config
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"encoding/json"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"path/filepath"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/log"
10: )
11: 
12: const (
13: 	// StateFileName is the name of the JSON state file within the config dir.
14: 	StateFileName = "state.json"
15: 	// InstancesFileName is the legacy per-file instances store name.
16: 	InstancesFileName = "instances.json"
17: )
18: 
19: // InstanceStorage is the interface for reading and writing serialised instance data.
20: type InstanceStorage interface {
21: 	// SaveInstances persists the raw instance JSON blob.
22: 	SaveInstances(instancesJSON json.RawMessage) error
23: 	// GetInstances returns the current raw instance JSON blob.
24: 	GetInstances() json.RawMessage
25: 	// DeleteAllInstances resets the stored instances to an empty list.
26: 	DeleteAllInstances() error
27: }
28: 
29: // AppState is the interface for reading and writing application-level state.
30: type AppState interface {
31: 	// GetHelpScreensSeen returns the bitmask of help screens that have been shown.
32: 	GetHelpScreensSeen() uint32
33: 	// SetHelpScreensSeen stores an updated bitmask and persists it.
34: 	SetHelpScreensSeen(seen uint32) error
35: }
36: 
37: // StateManager is the unified interface combining instance storage and app state.
38: type StateManager interface {
39: 	InstanceStorage
40: 	AppState
41: }
42: 
43: // State is the on-disk representation of application state.
44: type State struct {
45: 	// HelpScreensSeen is a bitmask tracking which help screens the user has seen.
46: 	HelpScreensSeen uint32 `json:"help_screens_seen"`
47: 	// InstancesData holds the serialised instance list as a raw JSON value.
48: 	InstancesData json.RawMessage `json:"instances"`
49: }
50: 
51: // DefaultState returns an initial State with no help screens seen and an empty instances list.
52: func DefaultState() *State {
53: 	return &State{
54: 		HelpScreensSeen: 0,
55: 		InstancesData:   json.RawMessage("[]"),
56: 	}
57: }
58: 
59: // LoadState reads state.json from the config directory. When the file is absent it
60: // creates and persists a default. On parse errors it returns a default without saving.
61: func LoadState() *State {
62: 	dir, err := GetConfigDir()
63: 	if err != nil {
64: 		log.ErrorLog.Printf("failed to get config directory: %v", err)
65: 		return DefaultState()
66: 	}
67: 
68: 	data, readErr := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, StateFileName))
69: 	if readErr != nil {
70: 		if os.IsNotExist(readErr) {
71: 			def := DefaultState()
72: 			if saveErr := SaveState(def); saveErr != nil {
73: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("failed to save default state: %v", saveErr)
74: 			}
75: 			return def
76: 		}
77: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("failed to get state file: %v", readErr)
78: 		return DefaultState()
79: 	}
80: 
81: 	var s State
82: 	if unmarshalErr := json.Unmarshal(data, &s); unmarshalErr != nil {
83: 		log.ErrorLog.Printf("failed to parse state file: %v", unmarshalErr)
84: 		return DefaultState()
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	return &s
88: }
89: 
90: // SaveState serialises s as indented JSON and writes it to the config directory.
91: func SaveState(s *State) error {
92: 	dir, err := GetConfigDir()
93: 	if err != nil {
94: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get config directory: %w", err)
95: 	}
96: 	if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); mkErr != nil {
97: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create config directory: %w", mkErr)
98: 	}
99: 	data, marshalErr := json.MarshalIndent(s, "", "  ")
100: 	if marshalErr != nil {
101: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal state: %w", marshalErr)
102: 	}
103: 	return os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, StateFileName), data, 0644)
104: }
105: 
106: // SaveInstances implements InstanceStorage: replaces the stored instances and persists.
107: func (s *State) SaveInstances(instancesJSON json.RawMessage) error {
108: 	s.InstancesData = instancesJSON
109: 	return SaveState(s)
110: }
111: 
112: // GetInstances implements InstanceStorage: returns the raw instance JSON blob.
113: func (s *State) GetInstances() json.RawMessage {
114: 	return s.InstancesData
115: }
116: 
117: // DeleteAllInstances implements InstanceStorage: resets instances to an empty list.
118: func (s *State) DeleteAllInstances() error {
119: 	s.InstancesData = json.RawMessage("[]")
120: 	return SaveState(s)
121: }
122: 
123: // GetHelpScreensSeen implements AppState: returns the seen-help-screens bitmask.
124: func (s *State) GetHelpScreensSeen() uint32 {
125: 	return s.HelpScreensSeen
126: }
127: 
128: // SetHelpScreensSeen implements AppState: stores an updated bitmask and persists.
129: func (s *State) SetHelpScreensSeen(seen uint32) error {
130: 	s.HelpScreensSeen = seen
131: 	return SaveState(s)
132: }

(End of file - total 132 lines)
</content>

99: 	assert.Len(t, events, 1)
100: }

def inject(task_num, injection, text):
